document_metadata.title: Paralympics 2024: Poppy Maskill and Matt Bush to be GB flagbearers at closing ceremony
document_metadata.first_words: # Maskill and Bush to be GB's flagbearers - Published Poppy Maskill and Matt Bush have been named as Great Britain's flagbearers for the Paralympics closing ceremony on Sunday. Maskill won three golds among a total of five medals, which is the joint most by a British athlete in Paris alongside swimming team-mate Alice Tai. The 19-year-old was victorious in the S14 100m butterfly - GB's first gold of the Games - before also winning the 100m backstroke and mixed 4x100m freestyle relay. She took

chunks.content: Poppy Maskill and Matt Bush have been named as Great Britain's flagbearers for the Paralympics closing ceremony on Sunday.
chunks.content_original: Poppy Maskill and Matt Bush have been named as Great Britain's flagbearers for the Paralympics closing ceremony on Sunday.

chunks.content: Maskill won three golds among a total of five medals, which is the joint most by a British athlete in Paris alongside swimming team-mate Alice Tai.
chunks.content_original: Maskill won three golds among a total of five medals, which is the joint most by a British athlete in Paris alongside swimming team-mate Alice Tai.

chunks.content: The 19-year-old was victorious in the S14 100m butterfly - GB's first gold of the Games - before also winning the 100m backstroke and mixed 4x100m freestyle relay.
chunks.content_original: The 19-year-old was victorious in the S14 100m butterfly - GB's first gold of the Games - before also winning the 100m backstroke and mixed 4x100m freestyle relay.

chunks.content: She took silver in both the 200m freestyle and the 200m individual medley.
chunks.content_original: She took silver in both the 200m freestyle and the 200m individual medley.

chunks.content: Para-taekwondo's Bush, 35, was a gold medallist in the men's K44 +80kg final.
chunks.content_original: Para-taekwondo's Bush, 35, was a gold medallist in the men's K44 +80kg final.

chunks.content: Both he and Maskill were making their Paralympic debuts in Paris.
chunks.content_original: Both he and Maskill were making their Paralympic debuts in Paris.

chunks.content: Maskill said: “Next time I really want to win five golds. I’ve had a really amazing time and I can't wait to carry the flag.“
chunks.content_original: Maskill said: “Next time I really want to win five golds. I’ve had a really amazing time and I can't wait to carry the flag.“

chunks.content: Bush, who was ruled out of the previous two Games with injury, said: “ParalympicsGB is such a close-knit team, it is extra special that I can carry the flag on behalf of everyone. It's an unbelievable way to finish my first experience of a Paralympic Games.“
chunks.content_original: Bush, who was ruled out of the previous two Games with injury, said: “ParalympicsGB is such a close-knit team, it is extra special that I can carry the flag on behalf of everyone. It's an unbelievable way to finish my first experience of a Paralympic Games.“

chunks.content: The closing ceremony, at Stade de France and featuring 24 artists from the French electronic music scene, is due to start at 19:30 BST.
chunks.content_original: The closing ceremony, at Stade de France and featuring 24 artists from the French electronic music scene, is due to start at 19:30 BST.

# Maskill and Bush to be GB's flagbearers

![Matt Bush and Poppy Maskill holding the Union flag behind them](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/675/cpsprodpb/ca8d/live/0b497490-6dbb-11ef-a713-d96a32d18a4e.jpg)Image source, ParalympicsGBImage caption, 

Matt Bush and Poppy Maskill were both making their Paralympic debuts in Paris

Alan JewellBBC Sport in Paris

- Published8 September 2024

**Poppy Maskill and Matt Bush have been named as Great Britain's flagbearers for the Paralympics closing ceremony on Sunday.**

Maskill won three golds among a total of five medals, which is the joint most by a British athlete in Paris alongside swimming team-mate Alice Tai.

The 19-year-old was victorious in the S14 100m butterfly - GB's first gold of the Games - before also winning the 100m backstroke and mixed 4x100m freestyle relay.

She took silver in both the 200m freestyle and the 200m individual medley.

Para-taekwondo's Bush, 35, was a gold medallist in the men's K44 +80kg final.

Both he and Maskill were making their Paralympic debuts in Paris.

Maskill said: "Next time I really want to win five golds. I’ve had a really amazing time and I can't wait to carry the flag."

Bush, who was ruled out of the previous two Games with injury, said: "ParalympicsGB is such a close-knit team, it is extra special that I can carry the flag on behalf of everyone. It's an unbelievable way to finish my first experience of a Paralympic Games."

The closing ceremony, at Stade de France and featuring 24 artists from the French electronic music scene, is due to start at 19:30 BST.
